name: Swanmore
overview: Swanmore is a rural village in the Meon Valley in south-east Hampshire, near Bishop's Waltham and on the edge of the South Downs National Park. It originally grew to house workers in local brickworks, so its housing is largely Victorian brick, with older properties in the hamlet of Upper Swanmore to the north. The Grade II listed Church of St Barnabas dates from 1844, and the village is unusual for its size in having its own secondary school, Swanmore College.
character: A quiet, tight-knit Victorian brick village with a strong community feel, well regarded for safety and its village amenities despite no longer having the shops it once did.
amenities: Village general shop and convenience store
Three pubs including The Rising Sun
Methodist church and St Barnabas parish church
Recreation ground with cricket and football, plus children's play area
Sports clubs and facilities (3G pitch at Swanmore College)

commutes.notes: Many residents commute to Portsmouth or Southampton
Public buses connect the village but are infrequent

prices.summary: Predominantly Victorian brick cottages and family homes, with larger detached properties in Upper Swanmore. Demand is supported by the in-village secondary school and South Downs setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks in the Meon Valley and South Downs National Park
Church of St Barnabas (Grade II listed)
Cricket and football at the recreation ground
Nearby Bishop's Waltham and its palace ruins
pros: Own secondary school (Swanmore College) plus primary in the village
Edge of the South Downs National Park
Good road access to Southampton and Portsmouth
cons: No railway station; car-dependent with infrequent buses
Fewer shops than in the village's past
Commutes to the coast cities take 30+ minutes
whoItSuits: Families wanting a quiet Hampshire village with its own schools and South Downs walking, who are happy to commute by car.
primarySupermarket: Central Convenience Stores in village; larger supermarkets in Bishop's Waltham

sources.label: A History of Swanmore - Local Histories
